zh.lifeSummary: 當Humphrey Cole於1439年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾卡爾內時，他的父親Alexander Coles30歲，母親Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle25歲。他於1464年與Agnes Harbottle在英國薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1558年11月19日在英國薩默塞特享年119歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特比曉普也斯 萊迪亞爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Humphrey Cole于1439年出生在英国英格兰維爾爾卡爾內时，他的父亲Alexander Coles30岁，母亲Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle25岁。他于1464年与Agnes Harbottle在英国薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1558年11月19日在英国薩默塞特享年119岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特比曉普也斯 萊迪亞爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Cole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Colton
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Nottinghamshire): habitational name from any of various places called Colton in England. Examples in Norfolk, Staffordshire, and North Yorkshire are from the Old English personal name Cola (or the cognate Old Norse Koli; see Cole 2) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. The place so named in Somerset has as its first element the Old English personal name Cūla (of uncertain origin). The one in Cumbria has a river name apparently derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘hazel’. This English name is also common in Ireland; it was the name of a bishop of Derry in 1397. There seems also to have been confusion with Culliton .
Scottish and Irish (Monaghan): shortened and altered form of Gaelic Mac Haldan, ‘son of Haldan’, see Haldane .

variants.name: Colclough
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Cheshire): habitational name from Cowclough in the parish of Whitworth, Lancashire, earlier recorded as Colleclogh, probably named with the Old English byname Cola (see Cole 2) + Old English clōh ‘ravine’.

variants.name: Calcote
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in Bedfordshire, Berkshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Northamptonshire, Warwickshire, and elsewhere) named Caldecote or Caldecott, from Old English cald ‘cold’ + cot ‘cottage, dwelling’. It has been suggested that in Old English this expression denoted an unattended shelter for wayfarers, although in fact some places with this name were of considerable status by 1086, when they appear in Domesday Book. In some instances this and some of the other shortened forms may have arisen from Calcot in Berkshire, Collacott(s) in Devon, or Calcutt in Wiltshire, in all of which the first element apparently comes from the Old English personal name Cola (see Cole 2) or the word col ‘(char)coal’, in which case the meaning would be something like ‘coalshed’.

variants.name: Collignon
meanings: French and Walloon: from a shortened pet form (see Cole and Colle ) of the French personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Colling
meanings: English (northern England and Devon): from the Old Norse personal name Kollungr, a derivative of Koli, or from Old English Colling, a derivative of Cola (see Cole ). Alternatively, from a pet form of the personal name Col(l), a pet form of Nicholas (see also Colin ).
German: variant of   Kolling   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Collard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Donat, Normand, Octave, Adelard, Aldor, Andre, Armand, Emile, Germaine, Marcel.
English (southern), French, and Walloon: from a diminutive of the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), a pet form of Nic(h)olas (see Cole ), formed with the Old French suffix -ard.

variants.name: Coale
meanings: English: post-medieval form of   Cole  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Coles
meanings: English (southern): variant of Cole , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Nichol
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from the Middle English personal name Nic(h)ol(e), a vernacular form of Nic(h)olas (see Nicholas ). Compare Nickel and Nicol .

In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Greek Nikolas , its variant Nicolas , or of any of various derivatives of the personal name Nikola(o)s ‘Nicholas’, including Nikolis (see Nicoli ). Compare Nicholas and Nicol .

variants.name: Coalson
meanings: English: patronymic from   Cole  .

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

variants.name: Humphry
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

variants.name: Humfrey

The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Humphrey Cole was born in 1439, in Calne, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Alexander Coles, was 30 and his mother, Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle, was 25. He married Agnes Harbottle in 1464, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 19 November 1558, in Somerset, England, at the age of 119, and was buried in Bishop's Lydeard, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Humphrey Cole est né en 1439, à Calne, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alexander Coles, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Harbottle en 1464, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 19 novembre 1558, à Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 119 ans, et a été enterré à Bishop's Lydeard, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat 1464 in Somerset, England Agnes Harbottle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 19. it.lifeSummary: Quando Humphrey Cole è nato il nel 1439 nel Calne, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Alexander Coles, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Harbottle nel 1464, nel Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 19 novembre 1558, nel Somerset, England, all'età di 119 anni, e fu sepolto a Bishop's Lydeard, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Humphrey Coleが1439年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアカルンで生まれた時，父Alexander Colesは30歳，母Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottleは25歳であった。1464年にイギリスソメルセトでAgnes Harbottleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1558年11月19日にイギリスソメルセトで119歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトビショプ'ス リディールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Humphrey Cole(이)가 1439년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 캘니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander Coles(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1464년 영국 서머세트에서 Agnes Harbottle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1558년 11월 19일 영국 서머세트에서 119세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 비숍'스 리디르드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Humphrey Cole nasceu em 1439, em Calne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Alexander Coles, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Harbottle em 1464, em Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de novembro de 1558, em Somerset, Inglaterra, com 119 anos, e foi sepultado em Bishop's Lydeard, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Humphrey Cole родился в 1439 г. в местности калн, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alexander Coles было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Harbottle в 1464 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 19 ноября 1558 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 119 лет и был похоронен в местности бишоп'с лидирд, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Humphrey Cole nació en 1439, en Calne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Alexander Coles, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Baroness of Fenwick Agnes Mullaly De Bottle, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Agnes Harbottle en 1464, en Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 19 de noviembre de 1558, en Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 119 años, y fue sepultado en Bishop's Lydeard, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
